claim.content: Define the Sommerfeld coefficient γ by γ = lim_{T→0} C(T)/T for the molar electronic specific heat C(T) of a metallic system at low temperature. The proposition is that, within the Fermi-liquid description applicable to heavy-fermion metals, the coefficient γ is proportional to the quasiparticle effective mass m* (i.e., γ ∝ m*), so that an observed enhancement of γ indicates a corresponding increase in the electronic quasiparticle effective mass or in the low-energy electronic density of states.
